behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (collectors -> aggregation payload), not single-function logic. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network needed to prove this behavior; source clients can be stubbed with deterministic payloads. LLM-DEP: assertion is on pre-summary input composition, not model output. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text is about "content passed to the summary-generation step," not real endpoint/network behavior; Agentic rejected because it does not evaluate summary quality or model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collection/orchestration modules with three stub source adapters returning distinct markers. Execute one report build and assert the payload sent into summary-generation contains all three source sections/values with correct mapping.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: This test requires cross-module wiring and payload handoff, which is exactly Integration.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: resilience behavior depends on interaction between fetch error handling, section rendering, and overall report assembly. REAL-INFRA: outage can be represented by adapter exception/timeout stub; real network is not required to expose this fallback bug. LLM-DEP: correctness is fallback control flow + section text insertion, not model judgment. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text specifies unavailable source handling outcome, which can be reproduced by in-process failure injection without real infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with two normal source adapters and one adapter that raises an availability error. Assert process does not abort, final report includes `data unavailable` note only for the failed section, and other sections remain populated.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs module interaction for partial-failure propagation, beyond Unit but below System.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on model output fidelity (whether generated narrative preserves key values), which a deterministic stub would mask. MOCK-HIDE: mocked summarizer always passing would hide real hallucination/omission failures. MULTI-STEP: this behavior is one model decision step, not a multi-agent chain, so Workflow is not required. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because behavior text explicitly requires "accurately represents" generated narrative content, i.e., model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fixed evaluation dataset of report inputs with known key values, call the real summary model, and assert extracted facts in generated narrative match expected key values within defined accuracy thresholds across repeated runs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test directly validates real model content quality, requiring Agentic level.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: requires real email transport/inbox behavior and networked delivery semantics; in-process mocks can hide SMTP/API auth, formatting, and deliverability issues. MOCK-MISS: end-to-end wiring across collectors, report assembly, and delivery must be exercised together. LLM-DEP: behavior text targets delivery to inbox "correctly," not summary quality. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected because the stated acceptance criterion is inbox delivery correctness, not evaluating model decision quality; Workflow rejected because no explicit requirement for multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script against a controlled real test environment (real endpoints or staging equivalents plus real email service/test inbox). Verify one run produces an email in the configured recipient inbox with expected subject/body sections and attachments if applicable.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Real transport and inbox verification makes System the minimum realistic boundary.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior depends on real filesystem persistence and retry across separate scheduled executions; mocks can miss path/permission/serialization/timing issues. MOCK-MISS: failure-handling handoff spans delivery failure detection, local persistence, and subsequent-run recovery. LLM-DEP: success criteria are persistence/retry semantics, not model output quality. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because behavior text explicitly requires save to "local file" and retry on "next scheduled run," both tied to real process/filesystem/runtime boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Run 1 with email endpoint intentionally unreachable; assert report file is actually written to disk with retriable metadata/state. Run 2 in a later scheduled invocation with email restored; assert saved report is retried and delivered, and retry state is cleared or marked sent.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real file persistence and cross-run behavior that Integration fakes commonly miss.
